MU.headline: HBM TAM pulled forward to $100B by CY2027; 16 SCAs with $22B+ deposits lock in demand; CapEx inflecting sharply as greenfield fabs start bits CY2028.

shifts.note: Q3 FY26 revenue $9.9B (+36% YoY); Q4 guided $10.7B; HBM driving the step-up
Gross margin 37.3% (GAAP) Q3; guided 42.5% Q4; start-up costs add $100-200M/q from Q4
shifts.trend: GM trough was ~22% Q4 FY23; now recovering through HBM mix

shifts.note: 16 non-cancellable SCAs; $22B+ customer deposits; HBM TAM $100B by CY2027 (pulled fwd from 2028)
shifts.trend: SCAs growing each quarter; NAND still oversupplied but DRAM/HBM tight

shifts.note: FY26 CapEx ~$27B; FY27 CapEx above low-to-mid-40s % of revenue; greenfield ID1/Tongluo/ID2
shifts.trend: FY25 ~$8B → FY26 ~$27B → FY27 >$40%+ of revenue

shifts.note: Q4 FY26 guided $10.7B ± $200M vs prior implied ~$9.5B; GM guided 42.5% vs prior ~38-40%
shifts.trend: Consistent upward revisions for 3 consecutive quarters

MU.verdict: MU is the purest HBM bottleneck play — 16 take-or-pay SCAs with $22B+ in customer deposits create quasi-contracted revenue through FY28. HBM TAM pulled forward to $100B in CY2027. The risk is greenfield construction: ~$27B FY26 and >40% of revenue FY27 with no bit output until CY2028, creating a 4-6 quarter margin drag from start-up costs. At current pricing, HBM gross margins are structurally above DRAM average, making gross margin expansion the primary investment driver. Current valuation (trades ~2x book) does not yet price in the FY27-28 earnings power.

NVDA.headline: Data Center $39.4B (+409% YoY); Networking $14.9B (+199%); Q2 guided ~$45B; Blackwell at full ramp; H20 China ban a manageable headwind.

shifts.note: DC ~$39.4B (+409% YoY) in Q1 FY27; Networking $14.9B standalone is now a $60B annualized stack
shifts.trend: Q4 FY26 DC ~$35.6B → Q1 FY27 ~$39.4B → Q2 guided ~$43B+

shifts.note: CoWoS-L packaging capacity at TSMC expanding; Blackwell GB200/NVL72 now at full production ramp
shifts.trend: Hopper constrained CY2023-24; Blackwell ramp completed by Q1 FY27

shifts.note: NVDA is fabless; CapEx is TSMC/CoWoS expansion (not on NVDA books); customer CapEx ($70B ORCL FY27) drives demand
shifts.trend: Hyperscaler CapEx commitments accelerating — structural demand floor

shifts.note: H20 ban adds ~$4.5B Q1 charge; H20 Lite and non-China mix partially offsets; export controls may tighten further
shifts.trend: H20 was $10-15B annualized China revenue stream now blocked

shifts.note: Q2 FY27 guided ~$45B; consensus had expected ~$43B; upside from Blackwell ramp and networking attach
shifts.trend: Every quarter since Q3 FY24 has beaten and raised

NVDA.verdict: NVDA remains the dominant AI GPU supplier with no credible near-term competitor for training and dense inference. Data Center at $39.4B in a single quarter represents ~$160B annualized — implying the stock trades at roughly 25x annualized DC revenue. The networking business ($14.9B, +199%) is becoming a second P&L within NVDA. Key risks: export control escalation (H20 ban expands), AMD MI400 ramp in CY2027, and potential custom silicon substitution by hyperscalers. Broadcom's $100B FY27 custom XPU guidance is the clearest evidence of share pressure at the margin.

shifts.trend: Latest filing introduces tightness language absent from prior
shifts.note: auto-derived (new tightness — single-quarter, lower confidence)

shifts.trend: Supply-tightness language present in both latest filings
shifts.note: auto-derived from filing keyword presence

HPE.covers: Q1 FY26 + Q2 FY26 filing window (through 2026-06-10)
HPE.headline: AI/DC networking orders >$2B cumulative by FY26 end; FY26 EPS raised 40%; triple-digit order growth; DRAM >60% of server BOM and supply-constrained.

shifts.note: AI/DC networking triple-digit YoY order growth; cumulative orders >$2B by FY26 end
shifts.trend: AI networking revenue inflecting from sub-$500M to >$2B cumulative in 18 months

shifts.note: FY26 EPS raised 40% vs original; AI networking mix richer than enterprise servers
shifts.trend: DC networking gross margins > overall HPE blended margin

shifts.note: DRAM >60% of server BOM and supply-constrained; constrains server shipments near-term
shifts.trend: DRAM tightness a headwind for server builds but tailwind for memory suppliers

shifts.note: FY26 EPS raised 40% vs original; FY27 networking guided +8-12% YoY
shifts.trend: Consistent upward revision driven by AI networking order inflow

HPE.verdict: HPE's AI networking inflection (Slingshot/EL8000) is tracking ahead of plan with triple-digit order growth. The pipeline being 'multiples of record backlog' is the strongest forward signal in the company's history. Risk: HPE remains exposed to traditional enterprise server cycles (constrained by DRAM supply) and execution risk integrating AI system components. FY27 networking +8-12% seems conservative given the backlog trajectory. The stock has re-rated but still trades at a discount to pure-play networking peers.

ORCL.headline: RPO $638B (+363%); $67B AI contracts in Q4 alone; OCI +93%; FY27 guided +34% CC with $70B net CapEx — Oracle is building the world's largest AI cloud.

shifts.note: FY26 revenue >$67B; OCI +93%; Q4 revenue $19.2B (+21%); Q1 FY27 guided +27-29%
shifts.trend: Cloud Infra: $8B → $16B+ run rate in 12 months; RPO $638B gives 9+ years of visibility

shifts.note: FY26 Net CapEx $48B; FY27 Net CapEx ~$70B; raising ~$40B capital for data center build
shifts.trend: CapEx tripling in 2 years as Oracle races to deploy GPU clusters for AI workloads

shifts.note: $67B AI contracts in a single quarter — diversified across multiple hyperscaler and sovereign AI customers
shifts.trend: RPO mix shifting toward AI training (large multi-year deals) vs traditional DB maintenance

shifts.note: FY27: +34% CC revenue; EPS $8.05; Net CapEx ~$70B — all above prior consensus
Raising ~$40B new capital for CapEx; debt load increasing significantly
shifts.trend: Balance sheet leverage rising to fund $70B CapEx while maintaining dividend

ORCL.verdict: Oracle's RPO of $638B (+363%) is the most striking data point in enterprise tech — it means ORCL has booked more contracted future revenue than its current market cap. GPU utilization at 97.5% confirms the AI cloud thesis is working. The risk is execution: $70B CapEx in FY27 requires flawless data center construction and GPU cluster deployment at unprecedented scale, while debt rises. At current valuation (~30x FY27E EPS), much of the inflection is priced in, but RPO durability and OCI margin expansion are the upside vectors.

AVGO.headline: AI semis $10.8B (+143%); FY26 raised to $56B; FY27 >$100B with ~10 GW XPU shipments; LTAs with Anthropic 5 GW, OpenAI 10 GW, Meta 3+ GW.

shifts.note: AI semis $10.8B Q2; Q3 guided ~$16B (+200% YoY); FY27 >$100B
shifts.trend: FY25 AI semis ~$12B → FY26 $56B → FY27 >$100B — 8x in 2 years

shifts.note: Non-GAAP GM 79% Q2; Q3 guided ~74% (mix shift to systems with lower GM than chips alone)
shifts.trend: GM compression expected as full-system integration (pods) becomes larger revenue share

shifts.note: 3 hyperscaler customers = ~90%+ of AI segment revenue; LOIs visible but highly concentrated
shifts.trend: Risk: loss of any one LTA would materially impair FY27 $100B guide

shifts.note: Custom XPU packaging (CoWoS-S at TSMC) is the binding constraint; LTAs lock capacity
shifts.trend: TSMC CoWoS capacity expansion the primary bottleneck for AVGO XPU shipment ramp

shifts.note: FY26 AI semis raised from $40B to $56B; FY27 >$100B first disclosed this quarter
shifts.trend: Every LTA disclosure has raised the floor; $100B is a floor not a ceiling

AVGO.verdict: Broadcom is the second AI silicon winner after NVIDIA — custom XPUs for hyperscalers at 10 GW scale in FY27 creates a $100B revenue stream with 79% gross margins on chips. The networking business (Tomahawk/Jericho) benefits from the same buildout. Key risk is customer concentration: 3 hyperscalers drive ~90%+ of AI revenue; any LTA renegotiation or in-house silicon success (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) would impair the guide. At ~25x FY27E EPS, the stock prices in near-perfect execution.

SNOW.headline: Product revenue $1.334B (+34%), NRR 126%, FY27 raised to $5.84B; Cortex Code 7,100+ accounts is fastest product adoption in company history.

shifts.note: Q1 +34% YoY; NRR recovery to 126%; FY27 raised from ~$5.6B to $5.84B
shifts.trend: NRR trough was 121% in FY25; recovery to 126% signals cohort expansion re-accelerating

shifts.note: Non-GAAP OM 12%; trajectory toward 20%+ medium-term; AWS partnership ($6B) improves unit economics
shifts.trend: Sales efficiency improving as Cortex/AI products scale with lower CAC

shifts.note: AWS $6B, OpenAI $200M partnerships provide multi-cloud anchor; reduces hyperscaler lock-in risk
shifts.trend: Iceberg open table format adoption broadens addressable compute base

shifts.note: FY27 product revenue raised to $5.84B (+31%); Q2 guided $1.415-$1.42B
shifts.trend: Q1 beat + raise establishes FY27 as a consistent upward revision story

SNOW.verdict: Snowflake's NRR recovery to 126% and Cortex Code's 7,100+ accounts validate the AI data layer thesis — enterprises are consolidating AI workloads onto the Snowflake platform rather than moving to competitors. The $5.84B FY27 guide implies ~31% growth at scale, which is exceptional. Risk: multiple still elevated at ~16x FY27E revenue; any growth deceleration hits the stock hard. AWS $6B partnership and OpenAI data deal create ecosystem lock-in that argues for a durable growth premium.

MRVL.headline: DC $1.83B; FY27 ~$11.5B (+40%), FY28 ~$16.5B (+45%), FY29 custom >$10B; Celestial AI acquisition adds scale-up optics (>$300M FY28); vertically integrating the AI fabric stack.

shifts.note: Q1 +28% total; DC mix growing as % of total; FY27-FY29 guide in consecutive 40-45% growth steps
Non-GAAP GM 58.9% Q1; Celestial AI integration may pressure GM near-term before scale
shifts.trend: DC-centric mix (custom silicon + PAM4 DSP) is GM-accretive vs legacy carrier products

shifts.note: 5 active hyperscaler custom silicon programs; scale-up optics + CXL switch acquisitions complete the AI interconnect stack
shifts.trend: Celestial AI (optics), XConn (CXL), Polariton (photonics) — 3 acquisitions in 6 months

shifts.note: M&A-driven CapEx; Celestial AI + XConn + Polariton = significant R&D integration investments
shifts.trend: MRVL transitioning from DSP/PHY vendor to full-stack AI interconnect platform

shifts.note: FY27 $11.5B and FY28 $16.5B guides raised vs prior; scale-up optics FY28 >$300M doubled
shifts.trend: Each quarter has introduced a new multi-year guide inflection point

MRVL.verdict: Marvell is executing a deliberate vertical integration of the AI network fabric: custom silicon (5 hyperscaler programs), scale-out networking (PAM4 DSP, coherent ZR), and now scale-up optical interconnect (Celestial AI) in a single company. The FY29 >$10B custom silicon guide — if realized — makes MRVL a $20B+ revenue company at 60%+ gross margins. Key risk: integration of 3 acquisitions simultaneously and reliance on 5 hyperscaler custom programs all progressing on schedule. At ~15x FY28E revenue, the stock prices in flawless execution.

CRM.headline: Q1 organic +7.7% (+13% with Informatica); CRPO +14%; FY27 $63B target (Rule of 50); Agentforce AWU 7x YoY — AI agents driving enterprise software re-monetization.

shifts.note: Organic +7.7% is below prior years but stabilizing; Informatica adds ~5pp; CRPO +14% suggests acceleration ahead
shifts.trend: FY25 ~+9% organic → FY26 Q1 ~+7.7% organic; Informatica closing the gap to Rule of 50

shifts.note: FCF $6.7B trailing; Rule of 50 = growth + FCF margin ≥50%; operating leverage improving
shifts.trend: CRM shifting from growth-at-all-costs to FCF-optimized model under Elliott pressure

shifts.note: Agentforce provides net-new monetization on top of existing seats — increases LTV per customer
shifts.trend: AWU pricing model is usage-based, not seat-based — structurally different from legacy SaaS

shifts.note: FY27 $63B reiterated; Rule of 50 target sets floor on capital return if growth disappoints
shifts.trend: Informatica integration risk is the main guide-miss vector

CRM.verdict: Salesforce's Agentforce pivot (7x AWU growth) is the right strategic move — usage-based AI agent pricing structurally expands LTV without requiring new seat negotiations. The CRPO +14% is the most important forward indicator: it suggests revenue growth should re-accelerate above organic 7.7% as Informatica integrates and agent workloads scale. At ~5x FY27E revenue, CRM is cheaper than SaaS peers but faces execution risk on Informatica integration and competitive pressure from Microsoft Copilot in the enterprise.

MOD.headline: FY26 DC revenue $1.1B (+73%); $4B LTA for DC cooling 2027-2029; FY27 DC guided +60-80%; Gentherm spin-off creates pure-play liquid cooling investment.

shifts.note: FY26 DC +73% YoY; Q4 DC >$400M (+158% YoY); FY27 guided +60-80% — growth accelerating into LTA ramp
shifts.trend: FY24 DC ~$350M → FY25 DC ~$640M → FY26 DC $1.1B → FY27 guide $1.7-2B

shifts.note: Q4 Adj. EPS $1.71 (+53%); FCF $153M; FY27 EBITDA guided $650-680M — significant margin expansion
shifts.trend: DC cooling margins structurally above auto thermal (being spun); mix improves post-Gentherm

shifts.note: $4B LTA with $165M upfront = hyperscaler locking in supply; liquid cooling capacity scarce
shifts.trend: LTAs are the new bottleneck signal: customers pre-paying for cooling capacity like HBM customers pre-paying for DRAM

shifts.note: Gentherm spin-off frees management focus and balance sheet for DC cooling CapEx
shifts.trend: Post-spin MOD becomes pure-play DC cooling with higher CapEx intensity acceptable to investors

shifts.note: FY27 total +20-35% YoY; DC +60-80%; EBITDA $650-680M — first time providing 2-year visibility
shifts.trend: $4B LTA provides unprecedented multi-year revenue visibility

MOD.verdict: Modine is the most underappreciated liquid cooling pure-play — $4B in contracted cooling revenue 2027-2029 with $165M upfront is the strongest demand signal in the sector after HBM SCAs. The Gentherm spin-off simplifies the story dramatically and removes automotive cyclicality from the multiple. At ~15x FY27E EBITDA, MOD is cheaper than peers VERTIV and NVENT on a per-unit-of-contracted-backlog basis. Key risk: customer concentration (the LTA appears to be with a single hyperscaler) and liquid cooling technology execution as deployments scale to 100kW+ per rack.

SNPS.headline: Q2 revenue $2.276B (+14%); Backlog $11B; FY26 raised to $9.625-9.705B; AgentEngineer 20 enterprise customers; Elliott active driving margin focus.

shifts.note: Revenue +14% YoY; DA segment growing; IP slightly down YoY (-6%) but recovering QoQ (+12%)
shifts.trend: EDA growth leveling at low teens as AI-driven chip complexity drives tool demand

shifts.note: Non-GAAP OM 39.5% Q2; FY26 guided ~41%; Elliott pressure accelerating efficiency focus
shifts.trend: SNPS transitioning from growth-at-all-costs to margin expansion story under activist oversight

shifts.note: FY26: Revenue $9.625-9.705B; OM ~41%; EPS $14.72-14.80; FCF ~$2B — all raised Q/Q
shifts.trend: Backlog $11B = >1 year of revenue visibility; renewal cycle locks in ACV growth

shifts.note: FCF ~$2B FY26; Elliott driving buyback and capital allocation discipline
shifts.trend: First time SNPS has committed to multi-year FCF targets under investor pressure

SNPS.verdict: Synopsys is the structural beneficiary of the AI chip design complexity wave — every custom XPU, memory controller, and photonic IC requires EDA tools, and SNPS has 50-60% market share in digital design automation. The AgentEngineer platform (20 customers, 30-50% PPA cycle reduction) is the next monetization layer. With $11B backlog and Elliott driving margins toward 41%+, the risk/reward is favorable. At ~30x FY26E EPS, the stock is not cheap but is defensible given the recurring backlog nature and structural demand from AI chip proliferation.

AMAT.headline: Systems >30% FY26 growth; e-beam crossing $1B; packaging >50%; EPIC center 10 partners; 8-quarter customer forecasts — longest supply visibility in history.

shifts.note: Systems >30% FY26; packaging >50%; e-beam >$1B new milestone; all driven by AI chip complexity
shifts.trend: WFE spending shifting from NAND to DRAM/advanced packaging/leading edge logic

shifts.note: Systems targeting mid-50s GMs; AGS (services) already there; blended GM marching from low-to-mid-50s
shifts.trend: EPIC co-dev model (10 partners) captures IP value earlier in the design cycle — richer margins

shifts.note: 8-quarter customer purchase forecasts locked; structural shift from spot to contracted; customers paying for capacity certainty
shifts.trend: Gate-all-around + backside power rail = 2x per-wafer content opportunity

shifts.note: No specific quarterly guide at conference; annual trajectory (>30% systems, e-beam >$1B) is the key signal
shifts.trend: AMAT consistent with prior guidance range; upside from packaging adoption acceleration

AMAT.verdict: Applied Materials is the broadest beneficiary of AI chip complexity — every new node (GAA, backside power), packaging advance (HBM, CoWoS, SoIC), and memory generation (HBM4, LPDDR6) requires AMAT etch, deposition, and inspection. The 8-quarter forward purchase forecasts are unprecedented and signal customers treating AMAT capacity as a strategic resource. At ~18x FY26E earnings, AMAT trades at a slight discount to ASML/KLAC — the discount reflects China exposure risk (30% of revenue) rather than fundamental weakness.

CSCO.headline: AI infrastructure orders $9B cumulative (from $5B); Acacia >$1B; 5 hyperscaler design wins in Q3; Silicon One roadmap to FY29; networking 'super cycle' thesis.

shifts.note: AI infra orders $9B cumulative (up from $5B prior); Acacia coherent optics >$1B annualized
shifts.trend: AI networking orders growing faster than internal forecast; each update has raised the tally

shifts.note: 5 hyperscaler design wins in Q3 (2 optics, 3 system) — supply being locked in at design stage
shifts.trend: Networking super cycle: hyperscalers need 5-10x more bandwidth per cluster per generation

shifts.note: 3 scale-across enterprise wins alongside 5 hyperscaler wins — diversifying beyond cloud-only AI demand
shifts.trend: Enterprise AI refresh (agentic workloads, on-prem LLM) adding a second demand vector

shifts.note: No specific quarterly guide at conference; AI orders metric is the primary lead indicator
shifts.trend: Silicon One FY29 roadmap locks in hyperscaler switching pipeline for 3+ years

CSCO.verdict: Cisco is repositioning from legacy enterprise networking to AI infrastructure with genuine hyperscaler traction — $9B in AI orders and 5 hyperscaler design wins in a single quarter is material for a company of CSCO's scale. Acacia coherent optics (>$1B) positions CSCO in the scale-out interconnect race alongside Marvell and MACOM. At ~12x FY27E earnings, CSCO is the cheapest networking play — the discount reflects legacy enterprise deceleration and Splunk integration risk. If AI networking orders sustain $9B+/year trajectory, the multiple compresses rapidly.

OKLO.headline: Pre-revenue; Groves Aurora construction complete (229 days); targeting July 4 2026 criticality; Meta 1.2 GW nuclear campus; NVIDIA+LANL fuel validation.

shifts.note: Construction complete in record 229 days; criticality target July 4 2026 — first commercial revenue H2 2026
shifts.trend: Pipeline: Meta 1.2 GW + NVIDIA LANL + DoD; LOIs for subsequent Aurora units growing

shifts.note: FY26 CapEx $350-450M; Groves commissioning + Lingua Franca fuel fabrication scale-up
shifts.trend: CapEx front-loaded; each subsequent Aurora unit will cost less to build

shifts.note: NRC licensing proceeding ongoing; NVIDIA+LANL fuel simulation partnership accelerates NRC pathway
shifts.trend: Bipartisan support for advanced nuclear; NRC reform improving licensing timelines

shifts.note: FY26 OpEx $80-100M; CapEx $350-450M; no revenue until criticality achieved and grid connection
shifts.trend: Pre-revenue; valuation is entirely option value on commercial nuclear success

OKLO.verdict: Oklo is the highest-risk, highest-optionality play in the portfolio — July 4 criticality at Groves would be the first US commercial microreactor milestone since the 1970s. The Meta 1.2 GW and NVIDIA/LANL partnerships validate demand from AI data center customers who need 24-hour carbon-free power at 50-500 MW scale. At current valuation ($2.5B cash + option value on 1.2 GW+), the market is pricing in successful commercialization. Risk: nuclear commissioning is inherently unpredictable — delays, NRC objections, or fuel supply issues could push first revenue to 2027+.

CEG.headline: Adj. EPS $2.74 (+$0.60 YoY); FY26 $11-12 affirmed; >20% EPS CAGR through 2029; FCF 2028-29 $11.5-13B; Calpine ~$2/share accretion; nuclear powering AI data centers.

shifts.note: EPS CAGR >20% guided through 2029; driven by nuclear PPAs with hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Amazon)
shifts.trend: FY24 EPS ~$8 → FY26 $11-12 → FY27-29 >20% CAGR implies $15-20 range by 2029

shifts.note: Nuclear 92.3% capacity factor — top quartile; Crane Clean Energy Center (TMI Unit 1) restart adds carbon-free GWh
shifts.trend: Nuclear FCF margin is structural — no fuel cost exposure (uranium vs gas); improves with PPA repricing

shifts.note: FCF 2026-27 $8.4B; 2028-29 $11.5-13B — CapEx is maintenance + Calpine integration, not expansion
shifts.trend: Calpine natural gas fleet adds 5,000 MW PJM capacity with grid ancillary value

shifts.note: Hyperscaler nuclear PPAs: Microsoft (Crane/TMI), Google, Amazon — diversified across 3 hyperscalers
shifts.trend: Data center nuclear PPA market developing fast; CEG is the dominant supplier

shifts.note: FCF growing to $11.5-13B by 2028-29; dividend + buyback policy; Calpine accretion adds ~$2/share
shifts.trend: CEG transitioning to capital return story as nuclear PPAs provide FCF certainty

CEG.verdict: Constellation Energy is the cleanest expression of the AI power thesis — nuclear provides 24-hour carbon-free power at competitive cost, which is exactly what hyperscalers need for net-zero data center commitments. The >20% EPS CAGR through 2029 is underpinned by nuclear PPAs with Microsoft, Google, and Amazon at repriced rates (2-3x legacy). Calpine adds ~$2/share accretion and PJM grid position. At ~25x FY26E EPS, the stock has re-rated but the FCF growth to $11.5-13B by 2028-29 provides a durable re-rating catalyst as investors discount the 5-year FCF stream.
